title: bupleurum ranunculoides subvar. humile
intro: Bupleurum ranunculoides subvar. humile, also known as Hare's Ear, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiviral agent, which contribute to its various health benefits. The plant is traditionally used to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Hare's Ear can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal use, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
